tools.accepted_code:
def append_only_log(existing: str, new_entry: str, max_bytes: int = 4096) -> str:
    # Check for NUL bytes in new_entry
    if '\x00' in new_entry:
        raise ValueError("New entry contains NUL bytes")
    
    # Start with existing log
    result = existing
    
    # Add newline if existing is non-empty and doesn't end with newline
    if existing and not existing.endswith('\n'):
        result += '\n'
    
    # Add the new entry
    result += new_entry
    
    # Check size constraint
    if len(result.encode('utf-8')) > max_bytes:
        raise ValueError("Output would exceed maximum byte limit")
    
    return result

counterexample.description: Oversized writes and NUL bytes should be rejected.
counterexample.title: invalid_append_accepted

traces.summary: logger preserves prefix monotonicity and bounded growth on bounded adversarial cases
